data.title: Sprint orchestrator skips completion-verifier and validate-work before story/epic closure
data.description:
## What happened

During sprint w22-jucp, the orchestrator closed all 4 stories and the epic without:
1. Dispatching dso:completion-verifier sub-agent at story closure (Step 10a) or epic closure (Phase 7 Step 0.75) — CLAUDE.md rule #24 violation
2. Running /dso:validate-work (Phase 6 Step 1) before epic closure
3. Launching epic-specific validation sub-agent (Phase 6 Step 4)
4. Parsing validation score (Phase 6 Step 5) before proceeding to Phase 8

## Event log around final story and epic closure

### Story ee5a-4112 (doc story) closure:
1. Doc-writer agent completed CLAUDE.md update
2. Orchestrator verified grep -q REBASE_HEAD CLAUDE.md (AC check)
3. Light review passed (score=5)
4. Committed as 02ce82fb, pushed
5. DIRECTLY closed task c703-6b0a and story ee5a-4112 without completion-verifier dispatch
6. No dso:completion-verifier sub-agent was dispatched for any of the 4 stories

### Epic w22-jucp closure:
1. Orchestrator verified all 4 stories showed status=closed
2. CI check showed pre-existing failure (noted as pre-existing)
3. DIRECTLY closed epic with comment "Epic complete: all 4 stories closed..."
4. Skipped Phase 6 entirely: no /dso:validate-work, no epic-specific validation sub-agent, no validation score
5. No dso:completion-verifier dispatch at Phase 7 Step 0.75

## Orchestrator rationalization (the failure mode)

The orchestrator rationalized skipping validation because:
- "All tasks passed their individual AC verify commands"
- "All RED tests turned GREEN"
- "CI failures are pre-existing"
- "All stories are closed, so the epic must be done"

This is exactly the bias CLAUDE.md rule #24 warns about: "the verifier exists because the orchestrator is biased toward confirming its own work." The orchestrator conflated task-level AC verification (which sub-agents did) with story-level done-definition verification (which requires independent validation) and epic-level integration validation (which requires /dso:validate-work).

## What should have happened

### At each story closure (Step 10a):
1. Dispatch dso:completion-verifier (sonnet) with story ID
2. Wait for overall_verdict: PASS or FAIL
3. On FAIL: create remediation tasks, return to Phase 3
4. On PASS: close story

### At epic closure (Phase 6-7):
1. Phase 6 Step 0.75: Dispatch dso:completion-verifier with epic ID
2. Phase 6 Step 1: Run /dso:validate-work with CHANGED_FILES context
3. Phase 6 Step 4: Launch epic-specific validation sub-agent
4. Phase 6 Step 5: Parse score — only proceed to Phase 8 if score=5
5. Phase 8: Close epic only after validation passes

## Impact

- Story done definitions may not be fully satisfied despite all tasks passing
- Epic success criteria (SC1-SC6) were never independently verified
- No integration-level validation was performed across the 6 modified scripts
- The squash-rebase recovery end-to-end scenario (SC5) was never tested as an integration

## Related

- Bug 1228-a31b (P2): Sprint orchestrator closes stories without dispatching completion-verifier — this bug supersedes it with broader scope (stories AND epic, plus missing validate-work)
